_description: Parity test inputs: every JACS language binding must accept these inputs and produce structurally identical outputs. Exact bytes differ (crypto nonce) but field names, types, and verifiability must match.

expected_signed_document_fields._description: Every signed document must contain these fields (exact values vary)

expected_verification_result_fields._description: Every verification result must contain these fields

sign_message_invalid_json_behavior._description: Documents a known cross-language behavioral difference for sign_message with invalid JSON input. Python's sign_message takes any Python object and serializes it to JSON first (so a raw string becomes a valid JSON string value). Node's signMessage takes a JSON string directly and rejects invalid JSON. Both behaviors are correct for their API contracts.
sign_message_invalid_json_behavior.python: succeeds (PyObject -> serde_json::Value serialization wraps string as JSON string value)
sign_message_invalid_json_behavior.node: rejects (passes raw string to binding-core sign_message_json which expects valid JSON)
sign_message_invalid_json_behavior.go: rejects (passes raw string to C FFI which calls sign_message_json)
